title: The Theory of Everything Is a War of Perception
subtitle: A cleaned reading of Secret History #9
voice_policy: Direct argumentative prose derived from the lecture. Avoid repeated third-person framing. Do not impersonate first-person authorship except when quoting verified source material.
trace_policy: Paragraph-level transcript refs. Interpretive claims stay tied to exact source spans.
dek: A source-grounded reading of Jiang's attack on the scientific worldview: Big Bang, evolution, neuroscience, school, and transhumanism become parts of one material story that forgets divinity, fears death, and lets power reinvent reality.
thesis: The lecture begins by compressing modern science into three axioms: random, material, emergent. Then it breaks them. Dark energy becomes a magic patch for a broken Big Bang; evolution cannot explain the human rupture; neuroscience cannot explain personality, consciousness, or memory; school teaches a process that destroys creativity because the idea comes before the method. Against that, Jiang builds a participatory universe: great books, dreams, near-death experiences, psychedelics, Kant, Hegel, and quantum mechanics all point toward reality as vibration, information, and spirit. Humans exist because perfection cannot imagine; love returns to the Monad; death is release; hell is the storage place of hate. The political consequence is harsh: power needs people to fear death and worship material reality, so science becomes a tool for reinventing reality. The final war is not over resources. It is over perception.

opening.heading: Core Reading
opening.text: The theory of everything starts as the school story: the Big Bang creates matter, evolution creates humans, neuroscience creates identity, and all of it is random, material, and emergent. The lecture's answer is that this story is not complete enough to be reality. Dark energy is a cheat added to the equation. Human beings do not fit gradual evolution. Babies arrive with personalities before the world has had time to write them. Great ideas do not come from procedure; they arrive like transmissions. From there the lecture turns into a cosmology of reception: books are portals, memory is an imprint in the universe, humans exist because perfection cannot imagine, and power wins by teaching people to fear death and call the spiritual world unreal. The war is not over resources. It is over perception.

marks.text: random, material, and emergent
books are portals
humans exist because perfection cannot imagine
power wins by teaching people to fear death and call the spiritual world unreal
The war is not over resources. It is over perception.

beats.heading: Random, Material, Emergent
beats.summary: The lecture reconstructs the school cosmology and then starts breaking it at its three foundations.

paragraphs.text: The official story is clean because it moves in a line. First comes the Big Bang. Matter expands into stars, planets, black holes, and earth. Life evolves from plants and sea life into animals and finally humans. Human superiority comes through language, cooperation, stored knowledge, and accumulated technology. The brain filters experience into memory; memory becomes identity; identities combine into worldview and personality.

Then the line is named as ideology. The scientific worldview says reality is random, not designed; material, not spiritual; emergent, not top-down. Particles become atoms, atoms become matter, matter becomes people. Jiang's move is to treat those premises not as neutral facts but as the story modern people were taught to inhabit.

marks.text: random, not designed; material, not spiritual; emergent, not top-down

paragraphs.text: The first crack is cosmology. If the universe expanded from one Big Bang, Jiang says, the expansion should be cleaner than what astronomers observe. Dark energy then appears as the patch: no one knows what it is, but it must exist because otherwise the model breaks. The classroom analogy is brutal. A student gives the wrong answer to a math problem, adds dark energy, and declares the problem solved.

marks.text: adds dark energy, and declares the problem solved

paragraphs.text: The second crack is evolution. Jiang does not deny that evolution works for animals. He says the human case is the problem. The gap between ape and human is too large, the missing stretch of human time is too strange, and the lack of human species diversity does not fit the gradual model. Science becomes a paradigm that refuses to budge because it has no better model ready.

beats.heading: The Idea Comes First
beats.summary: The mind does not behave like school says it does: personality, memory, creativity, and discovery point toward reception rather than procedure.

paragraphs.text: The real problem is the human mind. If worldview filters memory, how do babies filter anything before they have a worldview? Why do children arrive with distinct personalities? Where does personality come from if it is not simply a mixture of the parents? Neuroscience can name synapses and brain regions, but Jiang says it cannot explain consciousness or even locate memory without finally saying, in effect, that the whole brain stores it.

marks.text: children arrive with distinct personalities

paragraphs.text: School gives the mind a process: research, hypothesis, experiment, data, outline, thesis, draft, revision. Jiang reverses the arrow. The great idea does not come at the end of the process. The final product comes first, then the person works backward to create the evidence, outline, draft, and explanation. Einstein daydreams; Watson dreams a staircase; the method is what makes inspiration legible afterward.

marks.text: The great idea does not come at the end of the process. The final product comes first

paragraphs.text: That is why his own writing and teaching matter as evidence. He describes lying around, walking, daydreaming, and then suddenly receiving pages of ideas. His wife says he works as if possessed. Teaching works the same way: he carries a framework, reads the class, and receives the articulation in real time. The point is not laziness. It is that creativity is reception.

marks.text: creativity is reception

paragraphs.text: A great book is placed in the same category. It is not decoration or homework; it is a portal into the universe. The scientific method still has a use, as a student points out: it helps present inspired ideas to other people. Jiang agrees. But presentation is not origin. Origin comes through imagination, intuition, dream, and what he calls channeling the divine. Near-death experiences then become the next evidence: people return from death with light, life review, love, forgiveness, and a changed life.

marks.text: a portal into the universe

beats.heading: Reality Needs a Participant
beats.summary: Near-death testimony, psychedelics, great books, Kant, Hegel, and quantum mechanics are made to converge on one claim: reality is participatory.

paragraphs.text: Near-death experiences are not alone. Psychedelics, meditation, ancient serpent imagery, Dante, Milton, and Homer all become evidence that different routes touch the same higher force. The cosmic serpent matters because cultures far apart see similar images. Dante matters because the Divine Comedy can look like a near-death report even when there is no evidence Dante had one. The lecture is not trying to prove the spirit world in a laboratory. It is accumulating convergences.

Kant supplies the philosophical door. We never see reality in itself. We see reality filtered through time and space, which means we hallucinate the world into phenomena. But that creates a problem: why do our private hallucinations agree? Hegel supplies Jiang's answer. The noumenal source is Geist, the spirit world. We share a world because the filters come from one source.

marks.text: We share a world because the filters come from one source.

paragraphs.text: Quantum mechanics then becomes the physical analogy. The atom is not ultimately a little solid thing. At depth, matter is vibration, fields, wave and particle. Observation is not passive looking; it collapses possibility into what can be touched and measured. Schrodinger's cat and Wigner's friend push the point further: the box has to be opened by the participant. Someone else's report does not collapse your world for you.

marks.text: Someone else's report does not collapse your world for you.

paragraphs.text: The student objection is the obvious one: quantum mechanics is quantum; humans are macro creatures. Jiang accepts the distinction but refuses the wall. Quantum mechanics is still the basis of reality. If everything is vibration, it is also information. The brain receives the universe, combines vibration with experience into memory, and sends memory back as an imprint. The internet analogy is exact in structure: you write into it, it stores you, and you receive from it.

marks.text: The brain receives the universe, combines vibration with experience into memory, and sends memory back as an imprint.

beats.heading: Perfection Cannot Imagine
beats.summary: The participatory model becomes a Dantean cosmology of Monad, imagination, free will, love, death, hell, pearls, and mirrors.

paragraphs.text: Because the model is hard, Jiang turns it into story, using Dante. The Monad is the source of everything. It vibrates, breathes, emanates, and creates lower dimensions until spirit slows enough to become matter. The universe wants newness, imagination, freshness. But the spirit world is perfect, eternal, and immutable. It cannot suffer, err, or be surprised. Therefore it cannot imagine.

marks.text: the spirit world is perfect, eternal, and immutable. It cannot suffer, err, or be surprised. Therefore it cannot imagine.

paragraphs.text: Humans solve the problem by having bodies. Bodies age, bleed, hurt, hate, fall down, and choose. Free will lets the universe have real imagination because obedience alone would produce no novelty. Love is the compass: the spark of the Monad inside the person, the magnet that draws the person back toward the source. Good feels good because the spark grows.

marks.text: Free will lets the universe have real imagination

paragraphs.text: Death is not the enemy in this structure. Death is release. It interrupts the loop of error, returns the person to the universe, and makes life review possible: all the pain caused, all the good done, all the wisdom missed while alive. Hate goes somewhere else. It cannot return to the perfect spirit world, so it creates lower dimensions. That is hell.

marks.text: Death is release.
Hate goes somewhere else. It cannot return to the perfect spirit world, so it creates lower dimensions. That is hell.

paragraphs.text: The images are Hindu pearls and Dantean mirrors. In Indra's house, every pearl reflects every other pearl, so one brightening spark brightens the whole field. In Dante, God is the candle and humans are mirrors. However far away the mirror is, the flame still burns inside it. The divine mission is to expand the consciousness of the universe by love and imagination.

marks.text: one brightening spark brightens the whole field
The divine mission is to expand the consciousness of the universe by love and imagination.

beats.heading: The Machine Wants Death to Be Terrifying
beats.summary: The cosmology becomes political: power needs people to forget God, fear death, worship matter, and coordinate through inverted religion.

paragraphs.text: If early religions knew the spiritual model, the next question is how people forgot it. Jiang's answer is heaven, earth, and hell. Heaven is spiritual consciousness; hell is mechanical machine. Humans stand between them and choose. Those who choose hell gain technology, and technology lets them conquer and control. But control requires obedience, and obedience requires fear of death.

marks.text: control requires obedience, and obedience requires fear of death

paragraphs.text: Science now gets its political function. Big Bang, evolution, and neuroscience make people forget the divinity inside them and fear death as the worst thing. Transhumanism is the next step: not salvation from death but an attempt to defeat death by uploading the brain to the internet and trapping the person here forever.

marks.text: make people forget the divinity inside them and fear death as the worst thing
not salvation from death but an attempt to defeat death by uploading the brain to the internet and trapping the person here forever

paragraphs.text: The power mechanism is secret society inside bureaucracy. Bureaucrats are lazy; secret societies can coordinate. But secrecy needs glue. Shared transgression creates mutual blackmail, and mutual blackmail keeps everyone loyal. Religion or eschatology then becomes a script that lets people coordinate without meeting openly.

marks.text: Shared transgression creates mutual blackmail
Religion or eschatology then becomes a script that lets people coordinate without meeting openly.

paragraphs.text: That script is inverted because the actors know they are doing evil. They cannot return to God without judgment, so they imagine a superior power who justifies transgression: Satan. The system has unity, polarity, and as-above-so-below reflection. If good exists, evil exists; if God exists, Satan exists. You cannot defeat God directly, but you can corrupt human beings until Satan becomes everything.

The world is inverted by making people worship the material: money, power, technology, science. God, religion, compassion, and love are stripped away. The lecture names the present as a war between heaven and hell in which power must make hell into heaven and heaven into hell.

marks.text: power must make hell into heaven and heaven into hell

beats.heading: The War Is Over Perception
beats.summary: The student questions force Jiang to clarify God, Satan, intervention, belief, science, charisma, flood, and why the heaven/hell model still matters if it is metaphorical.

paragraphs.text: The first student question presses the obvious problem: if Satan accumulates power and God does not interfere, why would God do nothing? Jiang answers through free will. God and Satan cannot intervene directly. The test is human: only when darkness becomes complete can the human light fully shine. When one person shines, the light reflects into everyone else.

marks.text: only when darkness becomes complete can the human light fully shine

paragraphs.text: The second student question asks whether God and Satan are real or two worlds, positive and negative. Jiang's answer is participatory: everything is vibration, and belief helps create reality. If everyone believes God exists, God exists; if everyone believes sin exists, sin exists. That is why the war is not over resources. It is over perception.

marks.text: belief helps create reality
the war is not over resources. It is over perception.

paragraphs.text: That makes space travel a symbolic weapon. If the moon and Mars once belonged to heaven, putting people there can make heaven look empty. A fake alien invasion could do the same thing with Satan. The line is severe: science is not about discovering reality; it is about reinventing reality in a way that serves power.

marks.text: science is not about discovering reality; it is about reinventing reality in a way that serves power

paragraphs.text: The final student question asks whether a person can manipulate belief so completely that others make him into God or Satan. Jiang says no, not directly. Historically, charismatic rulers claim to be God's representative or descendant, not God itself. The claim has to pass through channeling, descent, or representation because people still assume human equality.

The close is the semester frame. If people believe God is dead, the material world is real, the spiritual world is a lie, and death is the worst thing, then hell has won. But the cosmos is interconnected, so hell cannot win permanently. Suffering makes the earth itself rebel; flood resets the world; humanity renews. Even if God and Satan are metaphorical projections, they help explain the structure of the war.

marks.text: Suffering makes the earth itself rebel; flood resets the world; humanity renews.
the material world is real, the spiritual world is a lie, and death is the worst thing
Even if God and Satan are metaphorical projections, they help explain the structure of the war.

questions.question: I thought quantum mechanics can only be applied on the quantum level. We are macro creatures, right?
questions.answer: Jiang accepts the scale objection but says quantum mechanics is still the basis of reality. The point is not that human beings are tiny particles. The point is that if reality at base is vibration, then vibration is also information, and the brain participates in that informational field by receiving, forming, and imprinting memories.

questions.question: If God and Satan do not interfere in our world, why would God do nothing if Satan's power grows beyond God?
questions.answer: The answer is free will. God and Satan cannot intervene directly in the world, because direct interference would cancel the human test. Jiang says God trusts humans completely. Only in complete darkness can humans fully shine, and when one person shines, that light reflects into everyone else.

questions.question: Are God and Satan real, or are they two worlds, one positive and one negative?
questions.answer: Jiang answers through participation rather than literalism. Everything is vibrational, and humans participate in the universe. If everyone believes God exists, God exists; if everyone believes sin exists, sin exists. That is why the fight is over perception: the winning side is the side that teaches people what reality is.

questions.question: If people can make God exist by believing, can someone make everyone believe in him and become a god or Satan?
questions.answer: No, not directly. Jiang says historical rulers and charismatic leaders usually claim to be God's representative, God's channel, or descended from God, not God itself. The divine claim needs mediation because people still assume equality: one person cannot simply declare himself God and make the claim stick.

source_notes.text: The transcript contains several ASR artifacts, especially around technical terms such as noumenon and Schrodinger. The read preserves the lecture's argument while using standard spellings where the source context is clear.
